stories.summary: More than 100 injured across country after Russia launches nearly 700 drones and dozens of ballistic and cruise missilesEurope live – latest updatesRussia has carried out its deadliest attack against Ukraine this year, killing at least 17 people and injuring more than 100 in a wave of drone and missile strikes across the country.Nine people died in the southern port city of Odesa and four were killed in Kyiv, including a 12-year-old boy. stories.headline: Israel and Lebanon agree 10-day ceasefire, Trump says
stories.summary: Agreement could increase the chance of securing a permanent end to the war between the US and Iran

stories.headline: Israel agrees to 10-day Lebanon ceasefire
stories.summary: President Donald Trump said the deal would go into force Thursday evening, as Pakistani mediators worked to extend a U.S.-Iran ceasefire and arrange new talks.

stories.headline: Hezbollah strikes north Israel ahead of Lebanon ceasefire announcement
stories.summary: Firefighters worked to extinguish a fire after a rocket attack from Hezbollah hit Kiryat Shmona in northern Israel.

stories.headline: Trump says Israel and Lebanon agree to temporary ceasefire
stories.summary: Lebanese Prime Minister Nawaf Salam welcomes Donald Trump's announcement of 10-day truce that takes effect on Thursday.

stories.headline: Israel and Lebanon agree to 10-day ceasefire, Trump says in social media post
stories.summary: Trump had announced earlier on Thursday that the leaders of Israel and Lebanon would speak about halting the fighting, in what would have been the first direct conversation in more than 30 years.

stories.headline: After three years of war, Sudan army and RSF locked in military impasse

stories.headline: Trump Says He Expects There Will Be a Deal Between Israel, Lebanon
stories.summary: President Donald Trump says talks between Israel and Lebanon may happen over the next week or two. He speaks with reporters at the White House. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Potential targets: Moscow releases data about European firms making drones for Ukraine
stories.summary: Since the beginning of Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine, European countries have provided Kyiv with extensive financial and military aid, supplying the armed forces with weapons systems and ammunition.

stories.headline: 10-day Lebanon-Israel ceasefire announced by Trump
stories.summary: Israel and Lebanon have agreed to a 10-day ceasefire starting at 21:00 GMT on Thursday, said US President Donald Trump.

stories.headline: Jet fuel shortage: Why Iran war could ground flights in Europe

stories.headline: Budget Airline Halts LA Flights as High Jet Fuel Costs Spill Into US Market
stories.summary: Norse Atlantic ASA, a Norwegian budget airline, has cut all of its flights to and from Los Angeles, citing high costs of jet fuel as disruptions from the war in Iran continue to tighten oil supplies and send prices soaring.

stories.headline: Henry Paulson Says US Should Prepare for a ‘Vicious’ Bond Crash
stories.summary: Former Treasury Secretary Henry Paulson called on US authorities to prepare a back-up plan in order to avert a potential collapse in demand for Treasuries — an event that he warned would have “vicious” effects.

stories.summary: Stocks may be soaring again, but the war in Iran has started to pinch the finances of many Americans.

stories.headline: Is it worth stockpiling cash as phsyical currency makes comeback in Poland?
stories.summary: While the trend towards the complete digitalisation of payments prevailed not long ago, today central banks and governments say that physical money may prove indispensable in crisis situations.

stories.headline: Turkish police detain 162 people over online posts about school shootings
stories.summary: At least 16 people were injured in a shooting at a high school on Tuesday, before another nine were killed in a separate school shooting on Wednesday.

stories.headline: Naples bank robbers hold 25 people hostage then vanish through tunnel

stories.summary: Noel Dixon, Senior Macro Strategist at State Street, discussed investor sentiment and market dynamics despite heightened geopolitical uncertainty in the Middle East. He noted that while much positive news is already priced in, the rally can continue, particularly with a shift toward higher commodity prices creating differentiated winners and losers across regions. stories.headline: ECB’s Dolenc Says Falling Energy Prices Challenge Case for Hikes
stories.summary: Declining energy prices have moved the euro-zone economy back toward the European Central Bank’s base case and may mean interest-rate increases aren’t needed, according to Governing Council member Primoz Dolenc.

stories.headline: Paramount-Warner Merger Will 'Desecrate America': Kirsten Vangsness
stories.summary: Kirsten Vangsness, actress and writer, joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Markets." More than 3,000 Hollywood actors, directors and screenwriters have penned a letter opposing the $110 billion takeover of Warner Bros. Discovery by Paramount Skydance Corp., citing concerns about job losses and higher costs with fewer choices for consumers. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: See Pivot In Gold Trading: Suki Cooper
stories.summary: Suki Cooper, global head, commodities research at Standard Chartered Bank, joins Scarlet Fu on "Bloomberg Markets." Gold has fallen about 9% since the start of the war, with a liquidity squeeze in the early weeks of fighting leading investors to offload holdings and cover losses elsewhere. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: House Votes to Preserve Deportation Protections for Haitians, Rebuking Trump
stories.summary: The action was largely symbolic since the president would be all but certain to veto the bill, but the bipartisan vote reflected resistance within his own ranks on his signature issue.

stories.headline: Judge Again Halts Aboveground Construction on Trump Ballroom
stories.summary: A federal judge wrote that an exception he made for work on security features did not cover most of the construction on the larger ballroom project Trump has proposed.

stories.headline: Federal judge sets new limits on Trump ballroom construction
stories.summary: U.S. District Judge Richard Leon clarified that some national security work can continue while the overall project is halted. Trump attacked him on social media.

stories.headline: House votes to restore protections for Haitians, defying Trump
stories.summary: Ten House Republicans joined Democrats to oppose President Donald Trump on his immigration policy Thursday, voting to restore temporary protections for Haitians living in the United States.

Charles Schwab Corp. is likely to launch prediction markets linked to financial events as competitors including Robinhood Markets Inc. expand their presence in the space.

stories.headline: Panel Advances Trump’s Triumphal Arch, Even as Key Member Suggests Changes
stories.summary: The Commission of Fine Arts, a Trump-aligned advisory body, granted preliminary approval. But its vice chairman suggested losing statues atop the structure and other revisions before a final vote.

stories.headline: Trump says China has agreed not to send weapons to Iran
stories.summary: The president made the claim, which Beijing has yet to confirm, amid anger in China that its vessels could be caught up in a U.S. blockade targeting Iranian ports.

stories.headline: Spain approves plan to give around 500,000 undocumented migrants legal status
stories.summary: Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez said migrants helped "build the rich, open and diverse Spain that we are and to which we aspire".

stories.headline: Founder of China's Evergrande pleads guilty to fraud
stories.summary: Evergrande was once China's biggest real estate firm, with a stock market valuation of more than $50bn.

stories.headline: Israelis war-weary but most oppose Iran ceasefire, poll suggests

stories.headline: Italian PM condemns ally Trump over 'unacceptable' Pope criticism

stories.headline: UK could adopt EU single market rules under new legislation
stories.summary: The move has raised questions over parliamentary scrutiny of future rules to deliver planned EU deals.

stories.summary: Viktor Orbán's 16-year rule is over, defeated by a 45-year-old ex-party insider who convinced a majority of Hungarians to oust him.

stories.headline: Philippines accuses Chinese fishermen of dumping cyanide in South China Sea

stories.headline: Peru election drags into second day after ballot delivery fiasco
